title: dacryodes negrensis
intro: Dacryodes negrensis, also known as the Iroko tree, is a tropical plant species native to West Africa, characterized by its large trunk and spreading canopy. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its bark, which contains compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Dacryodes negrensis include its potential to treat fever, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in traditional medicine to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Dacryodes negrensis include a decoction of the bark, which can be used to treat various ailments, and is often administered orally or applied topically to wounds.
